reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. An attacker can trigger this vulnerability via checkpoint sync by providing a malicious state SSZ file with proposer_lookahead length != 64. The state is deserialized in configfork.go:171 via InitializeFromProtoUnsafeFulu (state_trie.go:649-652), which directly copies the protobuf field without length validation. When the node processes its first epoch boundary, ProcessProposerLookahead (transition.go:34-35) calculates lastEpochStart based on len(lookAhead) and performs a copy operation, causing out-of-bounds access or incorrect indexing. The validation in SetProposerLookahead:18-19 is bypassed because InitializeFromProtoUnsafeFulu does not call it. Attack path: checkpoint sync (UNTRUSTED P2P-like source) → SaveOrigin → UnmarshalBeaconState → InitializeFromProtoUnsafeFulu → ProcessEpoch → ProcessProposerLookahead → crash. 01e reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json, invariant 'Proposer lookahead length must always be (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD+1)*SLOTS_PER_EPOCH after epoch processing.' Severity: Medium. Prysm has 31% network share. Impact is >5% of network (satisfies Medium threshold of '>5% of the network'). Attack requires single malicious checkpoint state (single packet equivalent). Affects only Prysm nodes using compromised checkpoint providers, not the entire network.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Proposer lookahead length must always be (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD+1)*SLOTS_PER_EPOCH after epoch processing.
